title: bupleurum odontites var. glumaceum
intro: Bupleurum odontites var. glumaceum, also known as the yellow rampion or spring rampion, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Bupleurum genus, a group of flowering plants in the carrot family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of Bupleurum odontites var. glumaceum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headache and body aches, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
